mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate margins and triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, rising real yields amplify the negative impact of contracting consumer credit on equity valuations, producing strong negative forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS is in the top quartile of its historical distribution, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.10, or the signal predicts negative returns in more than 55% of positive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer credit outstanding exceeds the 90th percentile of its historical distribution, household deleveraging risk spikes, causing a sharp contraction in consumption-driven earnings and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS breaches the threshold, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate simultaneously increases signals overheating supply chains that will soon face capacity constraints, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, accelerating housing starts amplify the positive impact of rising GDP on construction and durable goods sectors, driving strong positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive when both HOUST and GDPC1 show accelerating growth, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% in out-of-sample testing.
